description_long: This is a cylindrical fire hydrant with a weathered red body and a beige top, showing signs of rust and paint chipping. The hydrant features two large side outlets and a smaller front-facing outlet, each capped with a round metal cover. The top of the hydrant is domed and fitted with a central operating nut, which is slightly rusted. The base is flanged and bolted, designed for secure attachment to the ground. The surface is rough and aged, with visible grime and discoloration, especially near the base and outlets. There are some embossed markings on the body, but no clear manufacturer name is visible.
description: A weathered red and beige fire hydrant with three capped outlets, a domed top, and a flanged base, showing signs of rust and aging.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main outlet and a side outlet, with the domed top and central nut clearly visible.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting both side outlets and the front outlet, with more detail on the flanged base and embossed markings.
description_view_2: Rear-side view showing the back of the hydrant, with one side outlet and the domed top, and more visible grime and discoloration.
description_view_3: Back view displaying the rear of the hydrant, both side outlets, and the flanged base, with the embossed markings more prominent.
